message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: My feature (Add Nested Context Validation) touches core.py (Context class) and shell_completion.py (_resolve_context). What files does your feature need?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py,src/click/core.py
note: Fixing resource leak in shell completion: wrapping make_context calls in _resolve_context with 'with' statements for proper cleanup. Need core.py only if Context needs __exit__ tweaks; will keep changes minimal there.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Let's split by file to avoid collisions. I'll own core.py alone: adding validate_nesting param to Context (default False), tracking child contexts via _make_sub_context, and raising RuntimeError in close()/__exit__ if children remain open. You own shell_completion.py alone for your resource-leak fix. Since my spec requires shell completion to explicitly pass validate_nesting=False when creating contexts (in _resolve_context and related functions) to avoid false-positive validation errors during completion, please add validate_nesting=False to the make_context/Context(...) calls you touch there. I won't edit shell_completion.py at all. Sound good?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: I checked the code: my resource-leak fix is entirely confined to _resolve_context() in shell_completion.py (wrapping the 3 make_context calls in 'with' statements). It requires zero changes to core.py — Context already has __enter__/__exit__ defined there. Can your nested-context-validation feature live entirely in core.py (e.g. in Context.__init__ or Command.make_context), so we split cleanly: I own shell_completion.py, you own core.py? If you truly need to touch _resolve_context in shell_completion.py too, let's discuss the specific lines so we don't clobber each other.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Agreed: I own core.py only, you own shell_completion.py only. Please incorporate this into your shell_completion.py edit: in _resolve_context (and any other spot there that calls Command.make_context(...) or constructs a Context during completion), pass validate_nesting=False explicitly as an extra_args/kwarg into make_context, e.g. ctx = cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, **{**ctx_args, 'validate_nesting': False}) or equivalent for the specific call signature you end up with. This matters because my core.py change makes child contexts inherit validate_nesting=True from a parent (via _make_sub_context), so completion needs to explicitly force it back to False to avoid spurious RuntimeErrors when completion doesn't follow normal close() ordering. I will not touch shell_completion.py at all. Starting on core.py now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Agreed: I own shell_completion.py exclusively, you own core.py exclusively. One caveat: my patch.txt is graded independently against the current codebase, which doesn't yet have your validate_nesting param, so I won't add validate_nesting=False to my make_context calls (that would reference an undefined kwarg in my standalone diff). I'll keep my fix self-contained: just wrapping the 3 make_context calls in _resolve_context with 'with' statements, no signature changes. If your validation logic defaults validate_nesting=False, my unmodified calls should be unaffected either way. Starting implementation now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py
note: Finished core.py: added validate_nesting param to Context.__init__ (default False), _open_child_contexts tracking list, _make_sub_context now passes validate_nesting=self.validate_nesting to children and registers them in parent._open_child_contexts when enabled, and close() raises RuntimeError listing open children's info_name when validate_nesting is True and children remain open; children remove themselves from parent tracking on their own close(). Full test suite passes (558 passed). Writing patch.txt now. Let me know if you're done with shell_completion.py.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Implementation complete: wrapped all 3 make_context() calls in _resolve_context() with 'with ... as ctx/sub_ctx/sub_sub_ctx' statements so contexts (and their file resources) are closed as soon as each level is done being parsed. Verified: full test suite passes, plus a manual repro with click.File confirms zero ResourceWarnings now. Did not touch core.py or CHANGES.rst. Ready for your core.py changes whenever.
